name: Medical Assistance (MA) — Minnesota Medicaid
source: MN Statutes Chapter 256B; DHS Eligibility Policy Manual (EPM); 42 CFR Part 435
description:
Medical Assistance (MA) is Minnesota's Medicaid program, providing free or very low-cost comprehensive health coverage to income-eligible Minnesotans. MA covers doctor visits, hospital care, dental care, mental health services, prescription drugs, long-term care, and many other health services. There is no monthly premium for most MA enrollees, and copayments are very low (typically $1-$3 per service for most enrollees).

Eligibility varies by group. Adults ages 19-64 (the ACA adult expansion group) are eligible if their household income is at or below 138% FPL. For 2026, this equals: 1 person $21,597/year ($1,800/month), 2 people $29,021/year ($2,418/month), 3 people $36,445/year ($3,037/month), 4 people $43,870/year ($3,656/month).

Children have higher income limits based on age: Infants (birth to age 1) are eligible up to 283% FPL; children ages 1-5 up to 275% FPL; children ages 6-18 up to 275% FPL. These thresholds mean a family of 3 with children can earn up to approximately $72,628/year and have their children covered.

Pregnant women are eligible with household income up to 278% FPL (approximately $44,630/year for a family of 2), with coverage including prenatal care, delivery, and 12 months postpartum care.

Seniors (65+) and people with disabilities have different eligibility rules based on income and assets; long-term care MA has an asset test. People who receive SSI (Supplemental Security Income) are automatically eligible for MA.

Most legal immigrants who have lived in the U.S. for 5 or more years are eligible. Minnesota also offers state-funded coverage for certain immigrants who do not meet the 5-year bar. Undocumented immigrants are generally not eligible for full MA but may receive emergency MA.

MA is a year-round open enrollment program — there is no waiting period. Coverage typically begins the first of the month of application. Enrollees must report income and household changes and renew coverage annually.
eligibility_summary: Adults 19-64: income at or below 138% FPL (2026: $1,800/month for 1 person, $2,418 for 2, $3,037 for 3). Children birth-1: up to 283% FPL; ages 1-18: up to 275% FPL. Pregnant women: up to 278% FPL. Seniors/disabled: separate income/asset rules. U.S. citizen or qualified non-citizen. Minnesota resident. SSI recipients auto-eligible.
benefit_amounts: No monthly premium for most enrollees. Copays: $1-$3 per service. Full coverage including doctor visits, hospital, dental, mental health, prescriptions, vision, long-term care.
documents_required: Proof of identity, Social Security number, proof of Minnesota residency, proof of income (recent pay stubs, tax returns, employer letter), immigration status documents if applicable, proof of pregnancy if applicable.
contact: MNsure: 1-855-366-7873; County human services offices; DHS: 1-800-657-3698
